action.reasoning: Observation: checkpoint sweep identified the remaining blocker: older strong_v1-style checkpoints are full-model CLIP finetune artifacts with a different classifier layout, and predict.py currently mis-infers that layout, causing size-mismatch errors. Analysis: with only one iteration left, the only rational move is final checkpoint archaeology rather than new training. We should make predict.py robust to the observed full-model formats by inferring classifier type from tensor shapes (including direct linear classifier cases), run the sweep once more on val, and if any recovered checkpoint beats the plain cached linear probe locally, submit it immediately; otherwise stop and preserve the existing best leaderboard score from iteration 1. Decision: patch predict.py for broader checkpoint compatibility, rerun the sweep, and submit only if a clearly stronger candidate is found.
